Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your customer information is accurate, accessible, and secure across all devices and team members. Singapore SMEs need this to comply with PDPA regulations, enable remote and mobile sales teams, and integrate with accounting software like Xero or QuickBooks. A typical scenario: your sales team uses WhatsApp to close deals while on-site, and customer data automatically syncs to your accountant's system for invoicing.

Sales Transactions.narrative: This family handles the core mechanics of selling — quotes, orders, invoicing, and commissions — so nothing falls through the cracks and your team gets paid fairly. Singapore SMEs rely on this to manage cash flow, track what's been sold, and keep sales reps motivated with transparent commission tracking. A typical scenario: you quote a customer on Monday, they order on Wednesday, you invoice on Friday, and your sales rep's commission is automatically calculated.

CRM & Relationship.narrative: This family keeps track of every conversation and opportunity with customers, so your team never loses a lead or forgets a follow-up. Singapore SMEs benefit from a shared view of the sales pipeline, so managers can forecast revenue and spot bottlenecks. A typical scenario: a prospect calls your sales rep, who logs the interaction; when the rep goes on leave, their manager can see the full history and keep the deal moving.

Marketing.narrative: This family automates customer outreach — email campaigns, website forms, social media, and feedback collection — so you can nurture leads and retain customers without manual effort. Singapore SMEs use this to build brand awareness, capture inbound leads from their website, and measure customer satisfaction. A typical scenario: a prospect fills out a form on your website, triggering an automated email sequence that educates them about your product until they are ready to talk to sales.

Commerce.narrative: This family powers online and omnichannel selling — e-commerce storefronts, marketplace integration, loyalty programs, and subscriptions — so you can reach customers wherever they shop. Singapore SMEs use this to sell on Shopee and Lazada without manual order entry, offer loyalty rewards to repeat customers, and manage recurring revenue from subscriptions. A typical scenario: a customer buys on your Shopee store, earns loyalty points, and receives a personalized discount email to encourage a repeat purchase.

Customer Service.narrative: This family organizes customer support tickets, tracks response times, and ensures no complaint is missed. Singapore SMEs use this to respond faster to customer issues, meet SLA commitments, and build a knowledge base so customers can self-serve. A typical scenario: a customer emails a complaint, it automatically becomes a ticket, gets routed to the right team member, and a reminder alert fires if it is not resolved within 24 hours.

Analytics.narrative: This family turns raw CRM data into dashboards and predictions — so you can see which deals are likely to close, which customers might churn, and where your sales process is slow. Singapore SMEs use this to make faster, data-driven decisions and spot trends before competitors do. A typical scenario: your dashboard shows that deals in the 'proposal' stage take 40 days to close, so you investigate and discover a bottleneck in your approval process.
